title: Conflict Diagnosis and Semantic Resolution · Graph Lens: Continue Abort
summary: Resolve an integration conflict by reconstructing both intents and verifying behavior rather than deleting markers until Git accepts the file. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git continue abort increment in a conflict reproduction, resolution, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use continue, skip, quit, and abort controls for merge, rebase, and cherry-pick with operation-specific state awareness.
Construct a new result from requirements, tests, and both histories rather than treating resolution as text selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Continue Abort
steps.instruction:
theory case — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=5, and repair limit=6 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Continue Abort contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use continue, skip, quit, and abort controls for merge, rebase, and cherry-pick with operation-specific state awareness.

content.text: Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=5, and repair limit=6 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

This does not satisfy Continue Abort for a conflict reproduction, resolution, and regression record.
Use continue, skip, quit, and abort controls for merge, rebase, and cherry-pick with operation-specific state awareness. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use continue, skip, quit, and abort controls for merge, rebase, and cherry-pick with operation-specific state awareness.

content.text: Use continue, skip, quit, and abort controls for merge, rebase, and cherry-pick with operation-specific state awareness. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Continue Abort while producing a conflict reproduction, resolution, and regression record.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=5, and repair limit=6 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue. Competency probe: Use continue, skip, quit, and abort controls for merge, rebase, and cherry-pick with operation-specific state awareness. A defensible solution applies Continue Abort, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a conflict reproduction, resolution, and regression record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-continue-abort — Use continue, skip, quit, and abort controls for merge, rebase, and cherry-pick with operation-specific state awareness.
base=$(git merge-base main repair)
printf 'base=%s\n' "$base"
git diff "$base"..repair
git ls-files --unmerged
# Resolve intent, stage, test, then continue.
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: git abort commands are interchangeable across every in-progress operation. This fails the Continue Abort contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use continue, skip, quit, and abort controls for merge, rebase, and cherry-pick with operation-specific state awareness.
